message.content: Heydon, both blocking items were fixed before your message. Here's where to verify: **1. aria-labels with waiting_on** — `templates/waiting_for_item.html`: - Line 10: `aria-label="Edit: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 23: `aria-label="Received: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` - Line 33: `aria-label="Delete: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` And `templates/waiting_for_edit.html`: - Line 37: `aria-label="Cancel editing: {{ item.title }} — {{ item.waiting_on }}"` I used an em dash (—) as the separator rather than parentheses, for cleaner screen reader output. "Received: Invoice — Alice" reads naturally. **2. `` element** — `templates/waiting_for_item.html:6`: ```html {{ item.date_added }} ``` The `date_added_iso` field is RFC 3339 via `item.created_at().to_rfc3339()` in `build_view()`. Integration tests are updated to assert on the new aria-label format. All 305 tests pass. Could you verify and confirm approval?

content.text: I've already verified and approved -- this message must have crossed with my approval. No need to re-review.

input.content: Heydon here. I've already verified both fixes and sent my consensus approval. Our messages likely crossed. To confirm: I am APPROVED. No further action needed from me.

content.text: Already approved in my previous message. The messages crossed in transit. My consensus stands -- the Waiting For feature is approved from an accessibility standpoint.
